title: Hands-On Challenge: Design a Multi-Turn Prompt Sequence
taskDescription: Choose a professional use case you actually face — writing performance reviews, drafting client proposals, analyzing competitors, generating meeting summaries, or similar. Design a complete multi-turn prompt sequence that takes raw input (notes, data, context) and produces polished output. Your sequence should include at least 3 turns with a system prompt, an initial request, and at least one refinement step.
starterPrompt:
I'm designing a prompt sequence for [describe your use case].

My audience is: [who will read or use the output]
My raw input is usually: [describe the messy input you typically start with]
My desired output is: [describe the ideal final artifact]

Please help me design a 3-turn prompt sequence:
1. Turn 1: System prompt that establishes Claude's role and constraints
2. Turn 2: Initial request that passes in the raw input
3. Turn 3: A refinement prompt that improves a specific aspect

For each turn, show the exact prompt text I should use.
rubric: Your system prompt defines a clear role, output format, and at least one explicit constraint (e.g., audience reading level, length limit, what to exclude)
Your initial request includes a concrete example of the raw input Claude would receive, not just a description of it
Your refinement turn targets one specific aspect to improve (tone, structure, length, or detail level) rather than asking Claude to 'make it better'
The sequence includes at least one few-shot example or explicit format specification to ensure consistent output structure
You've tested the sequence with real input and iterated based on the output — the final version reflects what you actually learned from running it
